q: Heat is the form of energy that can be transferred from one system to another as a result of ___.
___ deals with equilibrium states by outlining the relationship between energy and work.
Heat transfer concerns the generation, use, conversion, and exchange of ___ between physical systems.
One of the basic requirement for heat transfer is the presence of a ___ .
There are three basic ways of heat transfer; namely, heat conduction, heat convection and ___ .
In fluids, heat is mainly transferred by ___ , in which the motion of the fluid itself carries heat from one place to another.
In solid, heat is transferred by ___ process in which heat energy is transferred among the adjacent parts of the material without the motion of the material itself.
Heat radiation occurs in the form of ___ waves.
The individual parts of a medium do not move as a whole in ___ .
In heat conduction, heat energy is transferred by ___ distribution among the adjacent parts.
Metals have many ___ , which can carry heat.
The rate of heat conduction through a material of cross-sectional area $A$ is proportional to the ___ $\Delta T$.
The rate of heat conduction is inversely proportional to the ___ $l$ of the material.
___ $k$ is a measure of the ability of a material to conduct heat.
In SI units, $k$ is ___ ($W m^{-1} K^{-1}$).
The ratio $\frac{T_2-T_1}{l}$ is called ___ .
The rate of heat flow per unit area is proportional to the magnitude of ___ .
The mathematical model of heat conduction was first formulated by ___ .
The rate of heat flow per unit area is also known as ___ .
Negative sign in Fourier's law indicates that temperature $T$ must decrease as distance $x$ ___ .
In the SI units, heat current $H$ is ___ ($W$).
A high value for thermal conductivity indicates that the material is a good ___ .
A low value for thermal conductivity indicates that the material is a poor thermal conductor or ___ .
___ such as rubber, wood, and styrofoam are poor conductors of heat.
___ is the flow of heat through a fluid from places of higher temperature to places of lower temperature by movement of the fluid itself.
Convection currents are set up in the fluid because the hotter part of the fluid is not as ___ as the cooler part.
Convection occurs only in a liquid or a gas, never in a ___ .
___ force is what drives natural convection.
The basic equation for the rate of convection heat transfer is known as ___ .
In the equation for convection, $q$ is the ___ coefficient.
In SI units, $q$ is expressed ___ ($W m^{-2} K^{-1}$).
c: watt per metre squared per kelvin

q: Some of the weather conditions are created by ___ .
___ is a transfer of heat that does not require a material medium.
Energy transfer by radiation is the fastest (at the speed of light) and it suffers no ___ in a vacuum.
All bodies at a temperature above ___ emit thermal radiation.
Thermal radiation differs from other forms of electromagnetic radiation such as ___ that are not related to temperature.
A ___ object is a good absorber and a good radiator whose emissivity is close to 1.
A white, clear, or silver coated object is a poor absorber and a poor radiator whose ___ is much less than 1.
The emissivity of an object depends on the ___ of radiation.
The object which can absorb all the electromagnetic radiations falling upon it is called a ___ .
___ is the study of systems involving energy in the form of heat and work.
When two systems are in ___ equilibrium, there is no net heat transfer between them.
The ___ law of thermodynamics is a simple definition of thermodynamic equilibrium.
The first law of thermodynamics is an application of the ___ for heat and thermodynamic processes.
The change in internal energy $\Delta U$ of a system is equal to the net heat flow $Q$ into the system minus the total ___ $W$ done by the system.
$Q$ is positive if heat is ___ to the system.
$W$ is positive if work is done ___ the system.
The ___ $U$ of a system is defined as the sum of the energies of all the constituent particles.
The internal energy of ideal gases depends only on ___ , not on volume or pressure.
Internal energy of an ideal gas is directly proportional to the ___ .
___ and work are both energy in transit.
___ is a form of energy completely different from either heat or work.
A ___ may be defined as a change from one equilibrium state of a system to another.
In thermodynamics, a ___ process is a thermodynamic process that happens slowly enough for the system to remain in internal physical thermodynamic equilibrium.
An ___ process occurs at constant pressure.
In an isobaric process, the work is equal to the ___ of the shaded rectangle on a pressure Vs volume graph.
An ___ (isovolumetric) process occurs at constant volume.
In an isochoric process, no work is done by the system because $W = p \Delta V = 0$.
According to the first law, the change in internal energy must equal any ___ flowing into or out of the system in an isochoric process.
The symbol $c_v$ is ___ at constant volume.
The symbol $C_V$ is ___ at constant volume.
An ___ process occurs at a constant temperature.
For an ideal gas, internal energy $U$ is only a function of the ___ .
In an isothermal process, $\Delta U$ is equal to ___ .
In an ___ process, there is no heat transfer into or out of the system.
An adiabatic process can be done by enclosing the system in a ___ .
The first law of thermodynamics for an adiabatic process is reduced to $\Delta U = -W$.
The symbol $\gamma$ is the ratio of the ___ .
The temperature of a gas ___ when it is adiabatically compressed.
A process that does not satisfy reversible requirements is an ___ process.
___ is a system that converts heat to mechanical energy.
A series of processes in which a system is brought back to its initial state is called a ___ .
___ for a heat engine is the ratio of work output to heat input.
Applying the first law of thermodynamic to one complete cycle, there is no net change of ___ .
Internal combustion engines generate the input heat by the ___ of fuel within the engine itself.
A gasoline engine is called a ___ stroke engine.
In the ___ stroke, a mixture of gasoline vapour and air is drawn into the cylinder.
In the ___ stroke, the mixture of gasoline vapour and air is compressed until its pressure and temperature rise considerably.
Explosion of the hot mixture is caused to take place very rapidly by an ___ .
During the explosion process, the piston does not ___ .
In the ___ stroke, the hot combustion products expand and push the piston out.
An ___ allows some gas to escape until the pressure drops to that of the atmosphere.
In the ___ stroke, the piston pushes almost all the remaining combustion products out of the cylinder.
The ratio $\frac{V_1}{V_2}$ is the ___ .
The ___ engine induces only air into the combustion chamber on its intake stroke.
Diesel fuel is sprayed into the cylinder by the ___ and immediately ignites.
Gasoline engine is operating between petrol burning temperature 680 K and outside room temperature ___ .
In 1824, Nicolas Leonard Sadi Carnot described a hypothetical engine known as ___ .
The Carnot engine operates on an ideal ___ cycle between two heat reservoirs.
___ sets an upper limit on the efficiency of all other real engines.
In a ___ process, the system undergoing the process can be returned to its initial condition along the same path on the p-V diagram.
The most important condition for a process to be reversible is that the process should be carried out ___ slowly.
The Carnot cycle consists of four reversible processes; two isothermal processes and two ___ processes.
Step 1 of the Carnot cycle is reversible ___ expansion of the gas at the hot temperature $T_H$.
Step 2 of the Carnot cycle is reversible ___ expansion of the gas.
Step 3 of the Carnot cycle is reversible isothermal ___ of the gas at the cold temperature $T_C$.
Step 4 of the Carnot cycle is reversible adiabatic ___ of the gas.
___ states that no engine operating between two heat reservoirs can be more efficient than a Carnot engine operating between the same reservoirs.
The efficiency of a heat engine $\eta$ is defined as the ratio of the work output $W$ to the ___ $Q_H$.
The ratio of two temperatures $\frac{T_C}{T_H}$ is equal to the ratio of heat absorbed to ___ .
The temperatures of the reservoirs in Carnot efficiency equations are ___ temperature.
The radiation emitted by a blackbody is called ___ .
